content.text:
You are performing the FINAL review of the codebase, focused on a single dimension: UI/UX (ui-ux).

Review ALL changes made during this workflow through the lens of this dimension only, and report your findings. The complete diff of the changes is provided below.

## Changes made during this workflow
(No git diff was available — the working directory may not be a git repository, or there are no uncommitted changes against HEAD. Review the working tree directly.)

OUTPUT RULES:
- If this review dimension is NOT applicable to the changeset (for example: a UI/UX review when there are no UI-facing changes, or a security review when there is no security-relevant surface), set applicable=false, explain in notApplicableReason, and return an empty findings array.
- Otherwise set applicable=true and list every real finding, ordered by severity (critical first).
- Rate each finding: low (nit / cosmetic), medium (should fix), high (important), critical (must fix before merge).
- For every finding, write a complete `fixPrompt` that a fixer agent can execute directly and in isolation to resolve it: state the file(s), the exact problem, and the intended fix. Do not reference other findings.
- If there are genuinely no issues for this dimension, return applicable=true with an empty findings array — NEVER fabricate findings.
- Set the `dimension` field of your response to exactly "ui-ux".

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ dimension: The review dimension you assessed (must match the dimension assigned in the prompt), applicable: false when this review dimension does not apply to the changeset (e.g. a UI/UX review with no UI changes). When false, set notApplicableReason and return an empty findings array., notApplicableReason: Required when applicable=false. Explain why this dimension is irrelevant to the current changes. Empty string when applicable=true., summary: Overall assessment for this dimension (what you checked and your conclusion), findings: Array<{ id: Short stable kebab-case identifier, unique within this review (e.g. 'n-plus-one-user-query'), severity: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical" /* low = nit/cosmetic, medium = should fix, high = important, critical = must fix before merge */, file: Primary file the finding relates to, with line range if helpful (e.g. 'src/auth.ts:42-58'), title: Concise one-line summary of the finding, description: Detailed explanation: what is wrong, why it matters, and the intended correct behavior, fixPrompt: A complete, self-contained prompt that a fixer agent can execute directly to resolve this finding. Must state the file(s), the exact problem, and the intended fix. Do not reference other findings. }> /* Findings ordered by severity, most severe first. Empty when applicable=false or when there are genuinely no issues. */ }

# Software Developer Test Project 2026

## Project Goals

- **Spend a few hours:** Put your best foot forward, submit a project that shows your best work. The evaluation will be code reviews and discussions of your implementation and architecture choices.

- **Download the empty project:** [Here](#). We have started you with an index.html file and an empty controller. When you are done you can zip it up and share it with the team for review either as a download link or zip attachment to an email. If you share the project by email keep in mind the zip has exe files inside it and Gmail and other virus scanners may block the email. To avoid issues, rename the extension to something like `MyProject.CHANGEME`

- **End-to-End Flow that Works:** Implement both server side and client side logic in a primitive end to end flow that runs without errors for a few small features. The key goal is to show a working end to end proof of concept with enough C# and JavaScript code that we can get a feel for your coding style and have some interesting classes to discuss in the code review.

  - **Simple is better:** The less boilerplate junk the better - if you can pick a barebones project type or delete all the default controllers and files you are not using it will help us focus on your code

  - **Focus on Code:** Spend most of your time actually writing code so that we can have a substantial work product to review. For the purpose of this exercise we really need to see original code and are not interested in seeing a lot of framework or template usage.

## File & Directory Browsing Single Page App

The task is to create a web service API that allows users to query the contents of a directory on the web server and a single page web app that can be used to search and browse folders and files. A server side home directory should be configurable via variable.

Client side, the application should be deep-linkable (the state of the UI should be kept in the URL). All of the UI work should be done client side via JavaScript that renders HTML - do not render HTML server side. Styling is not as important for this task, we are more concerned with functionality. Build the UI using vanilla JavaScript or TypeScript (without React, Angular, or other UI library).

The goal is to produce a functional proof-of-concept to let us see how you think. It is important to provide a decent body of code, so that we can talk about your logic and choices. It isn't important to make the UI "pretty".

The point of this exercise is to end up with a body of work that is big enough that our team can review it and evaluate your design and coding style and skills.

## Requirements

- Ensure your solution builds in Visual Studio (any version 2022 or newer is acceptable), Rider, VS Code, or command line SDK tools
- Web API that allows Browse and Search Files & Folders and returns JSON
- Deep linkable URL pattern
- SPA (Single Page App using JavaScript)
- Upload/download files from the browser
- Show file and folder counts and sizes for the current view
- Build the UI using vanilla JavaScript or TypeScript (without React, Angular, or other UI library)

## Bonus

- Entire component contained in a dialog widget, with a trigger element (button, etc)
- Delete, move, copy files and folders
- Highly performant

Development Diary

1. Ran the Engin harness orchestrator (my own: https://github.com/harms-haus/engin, it uses pi-coding-agent with my own extensions to perform scouting, planning, parallel task implementation, and review) with the "develop" workflow to generate a base to start with.
2. Human Review: Fixed a few issues. Added icon pack (bootstrap). Cleaned up the dialog styling. Added an embedded mode so that the file browser appears in the browser's main page. The button remains and continues to open a dialog version. Added a sample set of files to browse around in.
3. Ran the Engin harness orchestrator with the "improve" workflow to clean up the codebase.
4. Found four distinct issues with the file service: Delete is broken on Windows (I use Arch btw); Upload doesn't check safe destination path; Windows control chars aren't caught by invalid chars; Symlinks allow escaping the sandbox.
5. Added CI/CD: GitHub Actions workflow. Switched to oxlint (faaast). Added prettier. Fixed prettier, linting, and test errors.

content.thinking: Now I have a clear picture. The work converted inline row action buttons (Download/Delete/Move/Copy) into a dropdown menu triggered by a "⋮" button, also accessible via right-click. There's also new CSS. Let me review the CSS changes and the broader context.

Now I have a clear understanding of the changes. Let me analyze the UI/UX aspects:

**Key Changes:**
1. The inline action buttons (Download/Delete/Move/Copy) have been moved into a dropdown menu (`.row-menu`) triggered by a "⋮" button (`.row-menu-btn`).
2. The menu can also be opened via right-click context menu anywhere on the row.
3. New CSS for `.browse-table`, `.row-menu-btn`, `.row-menu`, `.row-menu .btn`.
4. The "Actions" header label is now empty string `''`.

Let me assess the UI/UX quality:

**Accessibility (a11y):**
1. The "⋮" button has `aria-label="Actions"`, `aria-haspopup="true"`, `aria-expanded` toggling. But there is NO `aria-controls` attribute linking the button to the menu. Also, the menu itself isn't marked with `role="menu"` and the items aren't `role="menuitem"`. The arrow-key navigation expected for menus is not implemented. When the menu opens, focus is NOT moved to the first item, and the items themselves are keyboard-focusable but only via Tab (which is non-standard for menus).

2. The button is hidden via `opacity: 0` and `pointer-events: none` until row hover. This means keyboard users who tab through the table CANNOT see or reach the button (because `pointer-events: none` doesn't disable tab, but `opacity: 0` makes it invisible). Actually, opacity:0 still allows keyboard focus. But it would be invisible while focused. Wait — the selector `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']` shows it when expanded. But when tabbing onto it, aria-expanded is false, so it remains opacity 0 — invisible focus state. This is a SERIOUS accessibility problem: a focusable element that's invisible when focused.

3. Actually wait, also `pointer-events: none` is set when not shown. That doesn't affect keyboard. But the bigger issue: the trigger button is invisible (opacity:0) until hover. A keyboard-only user tabbing through can reach it but cannot see it. They'd have to expand it for it to become visible. This is a critical a11y issue (focus visibility / operability for keyboard users).

4. The menu items use `class="btn"` but they're a mix of `<a>` (Download) and `<button>` (Delete/Move/Copy). The Download link and buttons work via Tab in document order — so keyboard users can navigate the menu via Tab. That's acceptable-ish, but not the ARIA menu pattern.

5. Empty `<th>` for Actions column — this is an accessibility issue. An empty header cell provides no label for screen readers. Better to keep "Actions" text but visually-hidden, or use `aria-label`.

6. When the menu is opened via right-click (`contextmenu`), focus is NOT moved to the menu. Keyboard users who right-click (Shift+F10 or Menu key) won't get focus into the menu. Actually they don't trigger `contextmenu` via keyboard typically — but the focus management is missing.

7. `event.stopPropagation()` on the opening click — this prevents the document click handler from closing the menu. But there's a subtle issue: the document click handler closes on ANY next click, including clicking on a menu item. When you click "Delete", the menu item's click handler runs `window.confirm`, then the click bubbles to the document and closes the menu. This is intentional. OK.

**Responsiveness & Layout:**
1. The menu is positioned with `position: fixed` at `event.clientX/clientY` or `rect.left, rect.bottom`. If the menu opens near the right or bottom edge of the viewport, it will overflow and may be clipped or cause horizontal scroll. There's no clamping/flipping logic. This is a real UX bug — opening a menu near the right edge of the screen would push it off-screen. For right-click at the very bottom-right, the menu goes off-screen.

2. The "⋮" button has `padding: 2px 6px` and `font-size: 16px`. Touch target is roughly 20px tall x ~28px wide — that's WAY below the recommended 44x44px or even 24x24px minimum. This is a touch target accessibility issue.

3. Right-aligning columns 2-4 (Size, Modified, Actions) via `text-align: right` — the Actions column right-aligns the button too. Combined with the button being hidden until hover, this is okay.

**Visual Consistency:**
1. The dark theme colors `#2d2d30`, `#3c3c3c`, `#9a9a9a`, `#e6e6e6` match the existing dialog theme (VS Code dark). Good.

2. The three-dots character "⋮" (U+22EE Vertical Ellipsis) — this is a reasonable icon choice but may render inconsistently across fonts. Some fonts render it as a tiny set of dots. The dev-diary mentions they added bootstrap icons. They could have used a proper SVG icon. But "⋮" is widely supported.

3. Right-clicking a row opens the menu, but there's no visual affordance indicating rows are right-clickable. Discoverability issue — but the button provides the primary affordance.

**Interaction & Feedback:**
1. The menu does not close on scroll. Since the menu is `position: fixed`, if the user scrolls the `.results` container while the menu is open, the menu stays at its fixed viewport position, detached from the row. This is a UX issue (orphaned menu).

2. The menu does not close on window resize. Less critical.

3. When `rerender()` runs after a Delete/Move/Copy, the entire table is rebuilt. If another menu was open (shouldn't be due to one-menu invariant), it would be detached. OK.

4. The empty `<th>` header cell — screen readers will read an unlabeled column header. This is a real a11y issue.

**Forms & Input:** N/A (no forms added; window.prompt/confirm unchanged).

**Perceived Performance:** The menu is built per-row at render time. For large directories this adds extra DOM per row (4 extra elements per row: btn + menu + items). Not a big deal.

**Copy & Inclusivity:**
1. The aria-label "Actions" is generic. Multiple rows each have an identically-labeled "Actions" button — screen reader users navigating by name will hear "Actions, button" for every row with no context about which file's actions. Should include the file name, e.g., `Actions for "filename"`.

2. The destructive "Delete" action uses `window.confirm` which is appropriate.

Let me also check if there are existing tests that reveal expected behavior, and check render-browse.ts changes.

Let me also check the CSS test file to see what they're testing.

Let me look at the broader picture once more and identify the most significant findings.

**Most significant findings (ordered):**

1. **CRITICAL: Trigger button invisible when keyboard-focused.** The `.row-menu-btn` is `opacity: 0` until the row is `:hover` or `aria-expanded='true'`. A keyboard user tabbing to the button sees nothing (no visible focus). Worse, on touch devices there's no hover, so the button is permanently invisible/inaccessible via pointer. The `pointer-events: none` rule when not hovered also means touch users cannot tap the button at all — they have NO way to access the row actions. This makes the entire row-actions feature unusable on touch devices and invisible to keyboard users. This is critical.

2. **HIGH: Menu positioned with `position: fixed` at raw click coordinates with no edge clamping.** Near viewport edges (right/bottom), the menu overflows off-screen — clipped or causes horizontal scroll. Should clamp to viewport.

3. **HIGH: ARIA menu semantics incomplete.** The trigger lacks `aria-controls` (pointing to the menu id), the menu lacks `role="menu"`, items lack `role="menuitem"`, arrow-key navigation is not implemented, and focus is not moved into the menu on open. For a dropdown menu, the ARIA Authoring Practices Guide menu pattern is expected.

4. **HIGH: Empty Actions column header.** The header was changed from 'Actions' to '' — screen readers announce an unlabeled column. Should keep a visually-hidden "Actions" label or set `aria-label` on the `<th>`.

5. **MEDIUM: Menu does not close on scroll/resize.** Since the menu is `position: fixed`, scrolling the `.results` container (or page) leaves the menu floating at a stale viewport position, detached from its row. Should close on scroll (or reposition).

6. **MEDIUM: Touch target too small.** `.row-menu-btn` is `padding: 2px 6px`, `font-size: 16px`, `line-height: 1` → ~20px tall, below the WCAG-recommended 24×24 (and Apple/Google 44×44). Combined with the opacity issue, touch usability is poor.

7. **MEDIUM: Non-unique accessible names.** Every row's trigger has `aria-label="Actions"` with no file context, so screen reader users can't distinguish which row's actions they're operating. Should be e.g. `Actions for "<name>"`.

8. **LOW: No visible focus styling on the menu items / trigger.** The `.row-menu-btn:hover` and `.row-menu .btn:hover` rules style hover but not `:focus-visible`. Keyboard focus is not visually indicated on the trigger or items.

9. **LOW: Right-click discoverability.** The right-click context menu has no visual hint. (Minor; the button is the primary affordance.)

Let me prioritize: the critical one is the touch/keyboard accessibility of the trigger button. Let me also double-check the pointer-events: none rule — when not hovered, pointer-events:none means clicks pass through. So on a touch device, the user cannot tap the button. Combined with opacity:0, the button is effectively invisible on touch devices. This is critical.

Wait — actually, even on desktop, the button is opacity:0 until hover. A keyboard user tabbing to it would have aria-expanded=false, so it stays opacity:0. They cannot see what they're focused on. They could press Enter/Space to open it (which would set aria-expanded=true, making it visible). But the focus indicator on the button itself is invisible. That's a critical a11y issue (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance).

Actually, wait — let me re-examine. Is :focus-visible handled? No, only :hover and [aria-expanded='true']. So when focused via keyboard (before expanding), the button has opacity:0. Critical.

Let me also verify: the `.row-menu-btn` has `border: none` — so there's no default focus ring either. Even the browser's default focus outline might be there, but with opacity:0 the whole button including its outline is invisible. Yes critical.

Let me also verify there's no `:focus` styling that would override opacity. Looking at CSS: only `.browse-table tbody tr:hover .row-menu-btn` and `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']` set opacity:1. So keyboard focus without expansion = invisible.

This is a critical issue.

Let me write up the findings now. I'll focus on the most important ones with good fix prompts.

Let me also re-read the diff to render-browse.ts — the header changed from 'Actions' to ''. That confirms the empty header issue.

Let me also confirm there isn't a `:focus-visible` rule somewhere in app.css for `.row-menu-btn`.

I now have all the information I need. Let me confirm my findings and write the review.

Key UI/UX findings:

1. **CRITICAL — Trigger button is invisible to keyboard users and unusable on touch devices.** The `.row-menu-btn` is `opacity: 0` and `pointer-events: none` until the row is `:hover` or `aria-expanded='true'`. There is no `:focus` / `:focus-visible` rule. A keyboard user tabbing to the button gets no visible focus (and no way to know it's there before pressing it). On touch devices, there's no `:hover`, so the button is permanently invisible AND `pointer-events: none` makes it untappable — the entire row-actions feature is unreachable on touch. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard), 2.4.7 (Focus Visible), 2.5.1 Pointer Gestures / 2.5.5 Target Size.

2. **HIGH — Dropdown menu positioned at raw click coordinates with no viewport clamping.** When opened near the right or bottom edge (especially via right-click in the corner), the `position: fixed` menu overflows off-screen, gets clipped, and/or causes horizontal scroll. Need to clamp x/y to `window.innerWidth/Height - menu.offsetWidth/Height`.

3. **HIGH — ARIA menu pattern incomplete.** Trigger has `aria-haspopup`/`aria-expanded` but no `aria-controls` linking to the menu; menu has no `role="menu"`; items have no `role="menuitem"`; arrow-key navigation not implemented; focus not moved into menu on open. Screen-reader/keyboard users get a degraded menu experience.

4. **HIGH — Empty Actions column header.** `buildTable(['Name','Size','Modified',''])` produces an empty `<th>` for the actions column, so screen readers announce an unlabeled column header. Should keep a visually-hidden "Actions" label (e.g., a `.sr-only` span) or set `aria-label="Actions"` on that `<th>`.

5. **MEDIUM — Menu does not dismiss on scroll or viewport resize.** With `position: fixed`, scrolling the `.results` container (or resizing the window) leaves the menu floating at a stale position, detached from its row. Standard dropdown behavior is to close (or reposition) on scroll.

6. **MEDIUM — Non-unique accessible names.** Every row's trigger has the identical `aria-label="Actions"`. A screen-reader user tabbing through the table hears "Actions, button" for every row with no context about which file. Should be `Actions for "<name>"`.

7. **MEDIUM — Touch target too small.** `.row-menu-btn` is `padding: 2px 6px`, `font-size: 16px`, `line-height: 1` → ~20×28px, below the WCAG 2.5.5 (AAA) 44×44 and even the 2.5.8 (AA) 24×24 minimum.

8. **LOW — No `:focus-visible` styling on trigger or menu items.** Only `:hover` styles exist; keyboard focus on the trigger or on items like Delete/Move/Copy shows no visible focus indicator (the menu items rely on the browser default outline, which may be stripped by UA/reset elsewhere — actually nothing strips it here, but it's inconsistent with the rest of the app which has explicit `:focus`/`:focus-within` styles).

Let me write this up as JSON. I'll keep the most important 8 findings, ordered by severity. Let me make sure each fix prompt is complete and self-contained.

Let me double check whether the menu items would have a default focus outline. Looking at the CSS, I don't see any `outline: none` on `.row-menu .btn` or `.row-menu-btn`. There's `outline: none` on line 183 — let me check what that's for.

Let me finalize my findings. I'll order them by severity:

1. CRITICAL — trigger invisible/untappable for keyboard and touch users
2. HIGH — menu overflows viewport near edges (no clamping)
3. HIGH — ARIA menu pattern incomplete (role/controls/arrow keys/focus)
4. HIGH — empty Actions header is unlabeled
5. MEDIUM — menu not dismissed on scroll/resize

content.text:
Based on my review of the diff (the conversion of inline row action buttons into a "⋮" dropdown menu plus right-click context menu, with associated CSS and the empty Actions column header), I found several real UI/UX issues. The most severe is that the dropdown trigger is invisible and unusable for keyboard and touch users.

```json
{
  "dimension": "ui-ux",
  "applicable": true,
  "notApplicableReason": "",
  "summary": "The changeset converts the browse-table row actions (Download/Delete/Move/Copy) from inline buttons into a '⋮' dropdown triggered by hover-revealed button or right-click, plus new CSS and an empty Actions column header. I reviewed a11y, responsiveness/layout, interaction/feedback, and visual consistency. The interaction model has several genuine defects: the trigger button is invisible to keyboard users and completely unreachable on touch devices (opacity:0 + pointer-events:none with no :focus rule), the position:fixed menu overflows the viewport near edges with no clamping, ARIA menu semantics (role=menu/menuitem, aria-controls, arrow-key nav, focus management) are missing, the Actions column header is now an empty unlabeled <th>, and the menu does not dismiss on scroll/resize. Smaller issues: non-unique accessible names, undersized touch target, and no explicit :focus-visible styling.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "id": "row-menu-trigger-invisible-keyboard-touch",
      "severity": "critical",
      "file": "src/app.css:313-331",
      "title": "Row '⋮' trigger is invisible when keyboard-focused and unreachable on touch devices",
      "description": ".row-menu-btn is set to opacity:0 and pointer-events:none by default and only revealed by `.browse-table tbody tr:hover .row-menu-btn` or `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']`. There is no :focus/:focus-visible rule. Consequences: (1) A keyboard user tabbing to the button gets NO visible focus indicator (the button, including any outline, is fully transparent) until they happen to activate it — violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and 2.1.1 Keyboard. (2) On touch devices there is no :hover state at all, so the button stays opacity:0 AND pointer-events:none, meaning it cannot be tapped — the entire row-actions feature (Download/Delete/Move/Copy) is unreachable on phones/tablets, violating WCAG 2.5.1/2.5.8. The trigger must be perceivable and operable without a hover-capable pointer.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app.css, fix the .row-menu trigger so it is perceivable and operable for keyboard and touch users. Add a selector that reveals the button on keyboard focus, e.g. add `:focus-visible` (and/or `:focus-within` on the row) alongside the existing hover/aria-expanded reveal rule:\n\n.browse-table tbody tr:hover .row-menu-btn,\n.browse-table tbody tr:focus-within .row-menu-btn,\n.row-menu-btn:focus-visible,\n.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true'] {\n  opacity: 1;\n  pointer-events: auto;\n}\n\nAlso, so touch users can always reach row actions (devices have no :hover), make the button at least faintly visible by default — change the base `.row-menu-btn { opacity: 0; }` to a low but non-zero value such as `opacity: 0.35` (and keep it fully opaque on hover/focus/expanded), OR add a `@media (hover: none)` block that sets `.row-menu-btn { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; }`. Finally add a visible focus ring:\n\n.row-menu-btn:focus-visible {\n  outline: 2px solid #6aa6ff;\n  outline-offset: 2px;\n}\n\nDo not change the DOM structure or the open/close JS logic; only adjust the CSS reveal/focus rules."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-no-viewport-clamping",
      "severity": "high",
      "file": "src/app/dom-builders.ts:225-236",
      "title": "Dropdown menu positioned at raw click coords overflows the viewport near edges",
      "description": "openMenu(x, y) sets `menu.style.left = x + 'px'; menu.style.top = y + 'px'` with position:fixed and no clamping. When opened via right-click near the right or bottom edge of the viewport (or via the button when the row is at the bottom), the 140px-min-width menu overflows off-screen, gets clipped, and/or introduces a horizontal/vertical scrollbar — making action items (especially Delete/Copy on the right edge) partially or fully invisible and unclickable. Standard dropdown behavior flips/clamps the menu to stay within the viewport.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app/dom-builders.ts, in the `openMenu(x, y)` function inside makeRowMenu, after setting menu.hidden = false (so it has a measurable size) and before/after setting left/top, clamp the coordinates to the viewport. Read the menu's rendered size and the viewport, e.g.:\n\nconst rect = menu.getBoundingClientRect();\nconst maxX = window.innerWidth - rect.width - 4;\nconst maxY = window.innerHeight - rect.height - 4;\nmenu.style.left = Math.max(4, Math.min(x, maxX)) + 'px';\nmenu.style.top = Math.max(4, Math.min(y, maxY)) + 'px';\n\nMake sure to read getBoundingClientRect() AFTER revealing the menu (menu.hidden = false) so width/height are non-zero. Keep the position:fixed styling and the existing close-on-document-click/Escape behavior unchanged."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-aria-pattern-incomplete",
      "severity": "high",
      "file": "src/app/dom-builders.ts:131-245",
      "title": "ARIA menu pattern incomplete: no role=menu/menuitem, no aria-controls, no focus management, no arrow-key nav",
      "description": "The trigger uses aria-haspopup='true' and toggles aria-expanded, but: (1) it has no aria-controls pointing to the menu id, so assistive tech cannot associate the button with its popup; (2) the .row-menu container has no role='menu' and the items have no role='menuitem' (they are a plain div containing an <a> and <button>s), so screen readers do not announce a menu or menu-item semantics; (3) focus is not moved into the menu when it opens (neither the button click nor the contextmenu handler calls .focus()), so keyboard/screen-reader users are left on the trigger; (4) there is no Arrow Up/Down/Enter/Escape keyboard navigation between items (only default Tab order, which is non-standard for a menu). This degrades the experience for assistive-tech users below the expected ARIA Authoring Practices Guide menu-button/menu pattern.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app/dom-builders.ts makeRowMenu, complete the ARIA menu pattern. (a) Give the menu a stable id (e.g. `menu.id = 'row-menu-' + <unique per row>`) and set `btn.setAttribute('aria-controls', menu.id)`. (b) Set `menu.setAttribute('role', 'menu')`. (c) Set `role='menuitem'` on each appended action element (the Download <a> and each makeActionButton result). (d) On open (inside openMenu, after revealing the menu), move focus to the first menuitem: `const first = menu.querySelector('[role=menuitem]'); first?.focus();`. (e) Add a keydown handler on the menu that implements Arrow Down/Up to move focus among menuitems (wrap around), Enter/Space to activate the focused item (default behavior already does this for button/a, so mainly preventDefault for arrows and move focus), and Escape to close and return focus to the trigger button (call closeMenu() then btn.focus()). Keep the existing close-on-document-click and the existing item click handlers intact; this is purely additive aria attributes + focus/arrow-key handling. Update src/app/render-browse.test.ts only if any existing assertion checks attribute sets that would conflict (none currently do)."
    },
    {
      "id": "empty-actions-column-header",
      "severity": "high",
      "file": "src/app/render-browse.ts:31",
      "title": "Actions column header is now an empty unlabeled <th>",
      "description": "renderBrowse changed `buildTable(['Name','Size','Modified','Actions'])` to `buildTable(['Name','Size','Modified',''])`. buildTable sets `th.textContent = label`, so the fourth header cell is now a completely empty <th> with no accessible name. Screen readers announce an unlabeled column header (or skip it), so users navigating the table by header cannot identify that the column contains row actions. The header should still convey 'Actions' to assistive tech even if it is visually blank (to match the now-icon-only cells).",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app/render-browse.ts, restore an accessible label for the Actions column without showing visible text. Change the call back to pass a non-empty label but render it visually-hidden. Preferred approach: update buildTable in src/app/dom-builders.ts so that an empty-string header still produces an empty <th> (unchanged), and in renderBrowse pass 'Actions' but wrap it visually-hidden — simplest is to change renderBrowse to pass 'Actions' and then, after building the table, label the 4th th: `table.querySelector('thead th:last-child')?.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Actions')`. Alternatively, pass 'Actions' and append the text inside a span with the existing `.visually-hidden` class (defined in src/app.css). Either way the column header must announce 'Actions' to screen readers while remaining visually blank. Keep the empty visible appearance and the browse-table class."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-not-dismissed-on-scroll",
      "severity": "medium",
      "file": "src/app/dom-builders.ts:209-218",
      "title": "Open menu is not dismissed on scroll or viewport resize",
      "description": "The dropdown is position:fixed at viewport coords and its open state registers only document 'click' and 'keydown' (Escape) listeners. If the user scrolls the .results container (overflow:auto) or the page, or resizes the window while the menu is open, the menu stays pinned at its original viewport position, now detached from the row that owns it — confusing and potentially pointing at the wrong row's data. Standard dropdowns close (or re-anchor) on scroll/resize.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app/dom-builders.ts makeRowMenu, dismiss the menu on scroll and resize while it is open. In openMenu, after registering the existing document 'click' and 'keydown' listeners, also register a listener that closes the menu on any scroll (capturing, so it catches scrolls inside the .results overflow container as well as window scroll) and on window resize. E.g. add named handlers:\n\nfunction onScrollOrResize(): void { closeMenu(); }\nwindow.addEventListener('scroll', onScrollOrResize, true);\nwindow.addEventListener('resize', onScrollOrResize);\n\nand in closeMenu remove them with the same arguments (useCapture=true for scroll):\n\nwindow.removeEventListener('scroll', onScrollOrResize, true);\nwindow.removeEventListener('resize', onScrollOrResize);\n\nKeep the existing click/Escape behavior and listener teardown unchanged."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-non-unique-accessible-names",
      "severity": "medium",
      "file": "src/app/dom-builders.ts:140",
      "title": "Every row's trigger has the identical aria-label 'Actions' with no file context",
      "description": "btn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Actions') is the same for every row. A screen-reader user navigating the table by name or hearing the button announced hears 'Actions, button' for every row and cannot tell which file/folder's actions it controls. Accessible names for repeated controls should include the item they act on.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app/dom-builders.ts makeRowMenu (which receives `entry: FileEntry`), make the trigger's accessible name unique per row by including the entry name. Change `btn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Actions')` to `btn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Actions for ' + entry.name)`. Do not change any other attribute or the visible '⋮' textContent."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-touch-target-too-small",
      "severity": "medium",
      "file": "src/app.css:313-323",
      "title": "'⋮' trigger touch target (~20x28px) is below recommended minimum sizes",
      "description": ".row-menu-btn has padding: 2px 6px, font-size: 16px, line-height: 1, yielding a tap area of roughly 20px tall by ~28px wide. This is below the WCAG 2.5.8 (AA) 24x24 CSS px minimum target size and well below the 2.5.5 (AAA) / platform 44x44 recommendation, making the already hover-gated trigger hard to hit accurately on touch even once it is made visible. The tappable area should be enlarged.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app.css, increase the .row-menu-btn hit area to at least 24x24 (preferably ~32px) without changing its visual glyph size. Adjust the .row-menu-btn rule's padding so the box meets the minimum, e.g. set `padding: 6px 8px; min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;` (keeping font-size: 16px and line-height: 1) and ensure the button is centered in the actions cell (the cell is text-align:right from the existing rule, which is fine). Keep border:none, background:none, border-radius:4px, and the opacity/pointer-events behavior. This is a CSS-only change."
    },
    {
      "id": "row-menu-no-focus-visible-on-items",
      "severity": "low",
      "file": "src/app.css:361-372",
      "title": "No explicit :focus-visible styling on the trigger or menu items",
      "description": "Only :hover styles are defined for .row-menu-btn and .row-menu .btn (color/brightness/background on hover). There is no :focus-visible rule, so keyboard focus on the trigger or on the Delete/Move/Copy items relies on whatever the browser default outline is — inconsistent with the rest of the app, which uses explicit focus styling (e.g. .toolbar input:focus border-color:#6aa6ff, .toolbar label.btn:focus-within outline:2px solid #6aa6ff). For a consistent and reliable keyboard experience the menu should show the same focus affordance.",
      "fixPrompt": "In src/app.css, add explicit :focus-visible styling for the row menu trigger and items, matching the app's existing #6aa6ff focus accent. Add:\n\n.row-menu-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6aa6ff; outline-offset: 2px; }\n.row-menu .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6aa6ff; outline-offset: -2px; background: #3a3d41; }\n\nThis is additive CSS only; do not remove the existing :hover rules."
    }
  ]
}
```
